meta.book: Lay, Linear Algebra and Its Applications

preambles.text: Throughout these supplementary exercises, A and B represent square matrices of appropriate sizes.

items.label: 1a
items.text: If A is invertible and 1 is an eigenvalue for A, then 1 is also an eigenvalue of A^-1.

items.label: 1b
items.text: If A is row equivalent to the identity matrix I, then A is diagonalizable.

items.label: 1c
items.text: If A contains a row or column of zeros, then 0 is an eigenvalue of A.

items.label: 1d
items.text: Each eigenvalue of A is also an eigenvalue of A^2.

items.label: 1e
items.text: Each eigenvector of A is also an eigenvector of A^2.

items.label: 1f
items.text: Each eigenvector of an invertible matrix A is also an eigenvector of A^-1.

items.label: 1g
items.text: Eigenvalues must be nonzero scalars.

items.label: 1h
items.text: Eigenvectors must be nonzero vectors.

items.label: 1i
items.text: Two eigenvectors corresponding to the same eigenvalue are always linearly dependent.

items.label: 1j
items.text: Similar matrices always have exactly the same eigenvalues.

items.label: 1k
items.text: Similar matrices always have exactly the same eigenvectors.

items.label: 1l
items.text: The sum of two eigenvectors of a matrix A is also an eigenvector of A.

items.label: 1m
items.text: The eigenvalues of an upper triangular matrix A are exactly the nonzero entries on the diagonal of A.

items.label: 1n
items.text: The matrices A and A^T have the same eigenvalues, counting multiplicities.

items.label: 1o
items.text: If a 5×5 matrix A has fewer than 5 distinct eigenvalues, then A is not diagonalizable.

items.label: 1p
items.text: There exists a 2×2 matrix that has no eigenvectors in R^2.

items.label: 1q
items.text: If A is diagonalizable, then the columns of A are linearly independent.

items.label: 1r
items.text: A nonzero vector cannot correspond to two different eigenvalues of A.

items.label: 1s
items.text: A (square) matrix A is invertible if and only if there is a coordinate system in which the transformation x ↦ Ax is represented by a diagonal matrix.

items.label: 1t
items.text: If each vector e_j in the standard basis for R^n is an eigenvector of A, then A is a diagonal matrix.

items.label: 1u
items.text: If A is similar to a diagonalizable matrix B, then A is also diagonalizable.

items.label: 1v
items.text: If A and B are invertible n×n matrices, then AB is similar to BA.

items.label: 1w
items.text: An n×n matrix with n linearly independent eigenvectors is invertible.

items.label: 1x
items.text: If A is an n×n diagonalizable matrix, then each vector in R^n can be written as a linear combination of eigenvectors of A.
